text: {"analysis_summary": "Inspected the visible RTL under the provided scope, focusing on permission/security-sensitive interfaces: APB debug/control paths, interrupt controller registers, privilege/supervisor signals, and debug-unit access controls. The code contains several permission-related weaknesses or warnings: a directly exposed APB debug interface can halt/resume and manipulate debug state with no visible authentication/authorization gate; interrupt-controller APB registers are writable/readable without visible privilege checks while a security-mode input is unused; and the subsystem hardwires supervisor-mode output asserted, which may misrepresent privilege state to downstream logic.", "findings": [{"finding_id": "PERM-DEBUG-APB-UNAUTH", "status": "potential_warning", "summary": "APB debug interface appears unauthenticated and can halt/resume the core through always-accessible debug registers.", "vulnerability_category": "Missing authorization on debug/control interface", "affected_locations": [{"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/fc/fc_subsystem.sv", "line_start": 32, "line_end": 32, "module": "fc_subsystem", "signal_or_register": "apb_slave_debug"}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/fc/fc_subsystem.sv", "line_start": 301, "line_end": 308, "module": "fc_subsystem", "signal_or_register": "apb_slave_debug -> apb2per/debug path"}, {"file": "ips/zero-riscy/zeroriscy_debug_unit.sv", "line_start": 163, "line_end": 176, "module": "zeroriscy_debug_unit", "signal_or_register": "DBG_CTRL / debug_halt / debug_resume"}, {"file": "ips/zero-riscy/zeroriscy_debug_unit.sv", "line_start": 196, "line_end": 210, "module": "zeroriscy_debug_unit", "signal_or_register": "debug-only registers / regfile write path"}], "evidence": [{"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/fc/fc_subsystem.sv", "line_start": 32, "line_end": 32, "module": "fc_subsystem", "object": "APB_BUS.Slave apb_slave_debug", "evidence_type": "source_search", "description": "The fc_subsystem exposes an APB slave dedicated to debug access.", "supports_claim": "A memory-mapped/APB debug access path is part of the subsystem interface."}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/fc/fc_subsystem.sv", "line_start": 301, "line_end": 308, "module": "fc_subsystem", "object": "apb_slave_debug signal connections", "evidence_type": "source_search", "description": "The debug APB slave signals are wired directly into an APB-to-peripheral conversion path, including address, write data, write enable, select, enable, read data, ready, and error signals.", "supports_claim": "No visible authorization, lock, lifecycle, or privilege signal gates the APB debug path before it reaches the internal debug interface."}, {"file": "ips/zero-riscy/zeroriscy_debug_unit.sv", "line_start": 163, "line_end": 176, "module": "zeroriscy_debug_unit", "object": "DBG_CTRL access logic", "evidence_type": "source_search/read", "description": "The debug unit marks debug register bank 00_0000 as 'always accessible'. Within DBG_CTRL writes, debug_wdata_i[16] can request HALT when the core is not halted, and otherwise the same register can resume when halted.", "supports_claim": "A requester on the debug interface can halt or resume the core through always-accessible debug registers, with no visible permission check."}, {"file": "ips/zero-riscy/zeroriscy_debug_unit.sv", "line_start": 196, "line_end": 210, "module": "zeroriscy_debug_unit", "object": "debug-only register and GPR write access", "evidence_type": "source_search", "description": "The debug unit has additional debug-only register regions and general-purpose register write logic that become accessible once debug_halted_o is true.", "supports_claim": "After an unauthenticated halt, the requester may gain stronger control over debug-only state and register-file writes, subject only to debug_halted_o."}], "reasoning_summary": "The subsystem includes a dedicated APB debug slave and connects it directly to the internal debug path. In the debug unit, some debug registers are explicitly 'always accessible', and DBG_CTRL writes can trigger halt/resume behavior. Other sensitive debug functions are guarded only by debug_halted_o; however, debug_halted_o can itself be reached by the always-accessible halt request. No visible source evidence shows an authorization bit, secure lifecycle state, debug-lock, privilege level check, or external authentication gate on this path.", "security_impact": "An untrusted APB master with access to the debug slave could halt or resume the core and potentially escalate to reading/writing core state once halted. This can break execution integrity, availability, and confidentiality of register/CSR contents.", "confidence": "medium", "uncertainty_or_missing_evidence": "The visible scope does not include the full SoC APB interconnect or top-level access-control policy. The debug APB port may be physically isolated or externally protected outside this scope. Therefore this is reported as a potential warning rather than a fully confirmed exploit.", "recommended_follow_up": ["Confirm at the SoC integration level whether apb_slave_debug is reachable only by a trusted/debug-authenticated master; if not, add a debug authorization/lock gate before accepting debug_req_i/APB debug transactions.", "Require a lifecycle/debug-enable signal or secure unlock sequence before honoring DBG_CTRL halt/resume and before granting CSR/GPR access.", "Consider returning PSLVERR or withholding debug_gnt_o for unauthorized debug accesses rather than granting invalid/blocked accesses silently."]}, {"finding_id": "PERM-IRQ-REGS-NO-PRIVCHECK", "status": "potential_warning", "summary": "Interrupt-control APB registers are writable/readable without visible privilege or secure-mode enforcement despite security-related signals.", "vulnerability_category": "Missing privilege check on memory-mapped control registers", "affected_locations": [{"file": "ips/apb_interrupt_cntrl/apb_interrupt_cntrl.sv", "line_start": null, "line_end": null, "module": "apb_interrupt_cntrl", "signal_or_register": "core_secure_mode_i / ENA_SEC_IRQ / core_irq_sec_o"}, {"file": "ips/apb_interrupt_cntrl/apb_interrupt_cntrl.sv", "line_start": null, "line_end": null, "module": "apb_interrupt_cntrl", "signal_or_register": "r_mask, r_int, r_ack APB registers"}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/fc/fc_subsystem.sv", "line_start": 283, "line_end": 283, "module": "fc_subsystem", "signal_or_register": "core_secure_mode_i"}], "evidence": [{"file": "ips/apb_interrupt_cntrl/apb_interrupt_cntrl.sv", "line_start": null, "line_end": null, "module": "apb_interrupt_cntrl", "object": "security-related interrupt ports/parameter", "evidence_type": "source_read", "description": "The interrupt controller declares security-related parameter/input/output: ENA_SEC_IRQ, core_secure_mode_i, and core_irq_sec_o.", "supports_claim": "The module appears intended to support secure interrupt behavior or secure-mode distinction."}, {"file": "ips/apb_interrupt_cntrl/apb_interrupt_cntrl.sv", "line_start": null, "line_end": null, "module": "apb_interrupt_cntrl", "object": "proc_mask/proc_int/proc_ack APB write logic", "evidence_type": "source_read", "description": "Within the interrupt controller, APB writes update interrupt mask, interrupt pending, and acknowledgement registers based only on APB transaction decode and pwdata.", "supports_claim": "No visible privilege or secure-mode condition restricts APB writes to interrupt-control state."}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/fc/fc_subsystem.sv", "line_start": 283, "line_end": 283, "module": "fc_subsystem", "object": ".core_secure_mode_i ( 1'b0 )", "evidence_type": "source_search", "description": "The subsystem ties core_secure_mode_i to constant 1'b0 when instantiating the interrupt controller.", "supports_claim": "The integration does not provide a dynamic core security-mode indication to the interrupt controller."}, {"file": "ips/apb_interrupt_cntrl/apb_interrupt_cntrl.sv", "line_start": null, "line_end": null, "module": "apb_interrupt_cntrl", "object": "APB read path and response signals", "evidence_type": "source_read", "description": "The interrupt controller read path returns r_int, r_ack, r_mask, and FIFO event data on APB reads; pslverr is tied low and pready tied high.", "supports_claim": "All decoded APB accesses are accepted without visible access-denial signaling."}], "reasoning_summary": "The interrupt controller exposes APB-programmable mask, pending interrupt, acknowledgement, and FIFO state. The code updates these registers on APB writes without checking requester privilege or secure mode. Security-related signals/parameters exist, but the visible implementation does not use them to enforce access policy; at subsystem integration, core_secure_mode_i is tied low. If the APB slave is accessible to less-trusted software or bus masters, they can mask interrupts, forge pending interrupts, clear interrupts, or manipulate acknowledgements.", "security_impact": "A less-privileged bus master could disable or spoof interrupts, clear pending interrupts, interfere with event delivery, or cause denial of service/privilege-flow manipulation if it can access the APB interrupt controller.", "confidence": "medium", "uncertainty_or_missing_evidence": "The complete APB address decoder and bus-master trust model are outside the visible scope. The registers may be mapped only to trusted firmware in a larger design. Line-level evidence for every write statement is from the read_file output, which did not include line numbers.", "recommended_follow_up": ["Determine which masters can reach apb_slave_eu / the interrupt controller APB target in the complete SoC address map.", "Add explicit privilege/security checks for writes to interrupt mask, pending, ack, and FIFO-related registers when secure interrupt support is enabled.", "Use pslverr or ignore writes for unauthorized transactions, and consider read restrictions for sensitive interrupt/FIFO state."]}, {"finding_id": "PERM-SUPERVISOR-MODE-HARDWIRED", "status": "needs_more_evidence", "summary": "supervisor_mode_o is hardwired asserted, which may incorrectly grant privileged status to all subsystem activity.", "vulnerability_category": "Incorrect or constant privilege indication", "affected_locations": [{"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/fc/fc_subsystem.sv", "line_start": 44, "line_end": 44, "module": "fc_subsystem", "signal_or_register": "supervisor_mode_o"}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/fc/fc_subsystem.sv", "line_start": 273, "line_end": 273, "module": "fc_subsystem", "signal_or_register": "supervisor_mode_o"}, {"file": "ips/zero-riscy/zeroriscy_cs_registers.sv", "line_start": 284, "line_end": 295, "module": "zeroriscy_cs_registers", "signal_or_register": "mstatus.mpp / privilege state"}], "evidence": [{"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/fc/fc_subsystem.sv", "line_start": 44, "line_end": 44, "module": "fc_subsystem", "object": "output logic supervisor_mode_o", "evidence_type": "source_search", "description": "fc_subsystem has an output named supervisor_mode_o.", "supports_claim": "The subsystem exports a privilege/supervisor-mode indication to other logic."}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/fc/fc_subsystem.sv", "line_start": 273, "line_end": 273, "module": "fc_subsystem", "object": "assign supervisor_mode_o = 1'b1", "evidence_type": "source_search", "description": "The supervisor_mode_o output is assigned a constant 1'b1.", "supports_claim": "The privilege/supervisor indication does not reflect dynamic core execution mode."}, {"file": "ips/zero-riscy/zeroriscy_cs_registers.sv", "line_start": 284, "line_end": 295, "module": "zeroriscy_cs_registers", "object": "mstatus.mpp initialization/update", "evidence_type": "source_search", "description": "Zero-riscy CSR logic initializes/sets machine privilege-related mstatus.mpp fields to PRIV_LVL_M according to search results, and no occurrence of PRIV_LVL_U was found in zeroriscy_cs_registers.sv.", "supports_claim": "Visible CSR logic suggests limited or fixed privilege-mode behavior, making the exported supervisor indicator potentially a constant privilege assertion rather than a checked permission state."}], "reasoning_summary": "A signal named supervisor_mode_o is exported by fc_subsystem and hardwired to asserted. If downstream memory/firewall/peripheral logic uses this signal as a permission indicator, all accesses from this subsystem would appear privileged regardless of actual software state. The visible zero-riscy CSR logic also appears to hardwire machine privilege behavior rather than implement dynamic user/supervisor permissions.", "security_impact": "Potential privilege bypass in downstream access-control logic: accesses could be treated as supervisor/privileged unconditionally, allowing code running on the core to reach resources intended only for privileged contexts.", "confidence": "low", "uncertainty_or_missing_evidence": "No consumer of supervisor_mode_o is visible in the provided scope, so actual exploitability depends on external integration. It may be a benign tie-off if the core is always intended to run trusted machine-mode firmware or if the signal is unused.", "recommended_follow_up": ["Identify all consumers of supervisor_mode_o in the full design and determine whether it gates access to protected memory or peripherals.", "If supervisor_mode_o is used for authorization, drive it from real architectural privilege state instead of a constant.", "If the core intentionally runs only trusted machine-mode firmware, document that assumption and ensure untrusted code cannot execute on this core."]}], "no_finding_reason": "", "global_uncertainty": "The analysis is limited to files visible under the input scope and to tool outputs shown above. Several instantiated modules and the full SoC interconnect/address-map/access-control policy are not visible. Therefore, findings involving APB reachability or downstream consumers are classified as potential warnings or needing more evidence where appropriate."}
